comment: PRESENTER-FIRED EVENT FEED for the meal-week demo. day_offset is retained only to resolve each event to the fixed week's target ISO date at snapshot time; it does not decide when an event fires. The UI receives label/title/order as demo_events and sends trigger_event with an event_id. GoalAgent then runs one scoped LLM re-plan against that event's context+steer, deduped once by id.

events.label: Tue
events.title: Groceries arrive
events.summary: Fresh groceries arrived: 500 g chicken breast and 200 g spinach were added to the fridge.
events.steer: Change the Day 1 dinner to use the spinach before it expires, and drop items now in stock from the shopping list.

events.label: Wed
events.title: Paneer spoiled
events.summary: The paneer spoiled early and the family finished the leftover rice.
events.steer: Change the Day 2 dinner if it relied on paneer or rice, using in-stock ingredients, and add a sensible replacement to the shopping list.

events.label: Thu
events.title: Football practice
events.summary: Aarav has football practice 18:00-19:30 tonight, overlapping the usual dinner prep window.
events.steer: Change the Day 3 dinner plan so prep happens earlier, or swap it to a quicker dish that fits the compressed window.

events.label: Fri
events.title: Grandma joins
events.summary: Grandma is joining dinner tonight - one extra guest, and she needs a low-sodium meal.
events.steer: Scale the Day 4 dinner for one more person and keep it low-sodium.

events.label: Sat
events.title: Oven unavailable
events.summary: The oven kicked off a self-clean cycle and is unavailable this evening.
events.steer: Swap the Day 5 dinner if it is oven-baked, using a stovetop or no-cook option.

events.label: Sun
events.title: Lighter Sunday
events.summary: The family wants a lighter end-of-week dinner after a heavy week.
events.steer: Swap the Day 6 dinner to a lighter dish that cooks in under 30 minutes using in-stock ingredients.
